explanations.24: This command modifies the bash prompt (the text shown before you type) to include a whale emoji and color codes, using the TARGET variable we defined earlier.
explanations.45: The 'set -e -x' makes the script exit if any command fails and print each command before running it, helping catch errors.
explanations.46: This checks if DOCKER_UID is not equal to 1000; if true, it creates a new user with a custom ID instead of using the default.
explanations.66: NODE_ENV is being set to the TARGET variable value (either 'development' or 'production'), which tells Node.js how to run the application.
explanations.70: This creates a brand new Docusaurus project scaffolding in the directory, then immediately deletes some template files we don't need.
explanations.82: The '--immutable' flag means yarn will fail if the lock file doesn't match the package.json, ensuring everyone builds the exact same dependencies.
explanations.107: This copies the 'build' folder from the building_production stage into the nginx folder, but the syntax appears to be missing a closing quote after 'build'.
